name: Quinto - Codo - Belchite - Rodén — circular from Fuentes del Ebro
name_original: Quinto - Codo - Belchite - Rodén — circular desde Fuentes del Ebro
region_intro: Road cycling around Fuentes De Ebro offers varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Ebro and Ginel rivers, providing routes along river valleys and through agricultural lands. Gentle escarpments and reforested hills, such as Pinos del Calvario and Monte Valdepuy, offer elevated viewpoints and moderate climbs. The landscape also features historical sites like the Old Village of Rodén and former alabaster quarries, adding points of interest to cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Fuentes De Ebro The most popular road cycling route is Quinto - Codo - Belchite - Rodén — circular from Fuentes del Ebro, a 50.1 miles (80.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical villages and varied rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Gelsa and Pina de Ebro — circular from Quinto, an easy 29.5 miles (47.5 km) path. This route offers a gentle ride through the Ebro river valley. Local road cyclists also love the Old Village of Belchite – Old Village of Belchite loop from Fuentes de Ebro, a 69.6 miles (112.0 km) trail leading through historical sites and open countryside, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Fuentes De Ebro is defined by river valleys, gentle escarpments, and reforested hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Fuentes De Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuentes De Ebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Fuentes de Ebro?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes available around Fuentes de Ebro, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging options to explore the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ebro?
A: The terrain around Fuentes de Ebro is diverse, featuring routes along the Ebro and Ginel river valleys, gentle escarpments, and reforested hills like Pinos del Calvario and Monte Valdepuy. You can expect a mix of flat stretches and moderate climbs, with some routes offering panoramic views from elevated points such as old alabaster quarries.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Fuentes de Ebro?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy road cycling routes around Fuentes de Ebro. A great option for a gentle ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992647804/gelsa-and-pina-de-ebro-circular-from-quinto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gelsa and Pina de Ebro — circular from Quinto&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.5 miles (47.5 km) long and offers a pleasant ride through the Ebro river valley.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Old Village of Belchite loop from Fuentes de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 69.6 miles (112.0 km) trail that takes approximately 5 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route leads through historical sites and open countryside.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical and natural landmarks. You can explore the poignant ruins of the Old Village of Rodén, which bears witness to the Spanish Civil War. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfajarín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pina de Ebro Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features old alabaster quarries that offer unique geological features and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991232800/quinto-codo-belchite-roden-circular-from-fuentes-del-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinto - Codo - Belchite - Rodén — circular from Fuentes del Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 50.1 miles (80.6 km) and explores historical villages and varied rural landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints accessible by road bike?
A: Road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ebro offer several scenic viewpoints. From areas like Monte Valdepuy and the reforested alabaster quarries, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views of Fuentes' orchards, the Ebro river's AVE bridge, and surrounding agricultural lands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meanders Viewpoint – Sástago loop from Quinto&lt;/a&gt; is another route known for its vistas.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Fuentes de Ebro?
A: The road cycling routes around Fuentes de Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the mix of river valleys, gentle escarpments, and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any natural features to look out for while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its proximity to the Ebro and Ginel rivers, offering picturesque routes along their banks. You'll also encounter areas like Pinos del Calvario, a 28-hectare pine forest, and the gentle hills of Monte Valdepuy, both reforested and providing natural beauty. Keep an eye out for the unique geological formations of the old alabaster quarries.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fuentes de Ebro?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions like Fuentes de Ebro in Aragon, Spain, generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Church of San Miguel Arcángel?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St. Michael the Archangel – Sástago loop from Fuentes de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 80.2 km route that starts from Fuentes de Ebro, allowing you to pass by this notable landmark and explore the surrounding area.
tips: If there is something we must visit in Quinto, it is without a doubt the Museum of the Mummies located in the church. With or without a guided tour, we will fully delve into the history of the church, of Quinto and of the mummified bodies (good preservation) by nature that tell stories from another era.

We are in the town of Belchite and we have the option of making a stop (mandatory if we have never been) to walk the streets of the Old Town of Belchite (with reservation) learning about the events that took place during the days of the Battle of Belchite. We cannot miss it if we want to know our history.
Visit the website for schedules, prices and guided tours: belchite.es/visita-guiada-pueblo-viejo-diurna

We are on the road that leads to Rodén and from this point we will look at the Rodén Castle that stands on the hill of the old town of the same name, one of the 6 towns that were bombed during the Civil War and were not rebuilt.
If we are more interested, we can get closer to read the information panels in the area.
